news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Announces Late-Breaking Presentation of LEVEL Clinical Trial Data at ESC Congress 2026
news.summary: Tenax announced that topline results from the Phase 3 LEVEL trial of TNX-103 (oral levosimendan) in PH-HFpEF will be unveiled in a Late-Breaking Clinical Science session at the ESC Congress 2026 in Munich on August 29, and that the company now expects to report data in August 2026 as it proceeds toward database lock and statistical analysis.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Announces Inducement Grants Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
news.summary: Tenax granted non-qualified stock options covering an aggregate of 160,000 shares as material inducements to three newly hired non-executive employees in accounting, clinical operations and CMC/product development roles, as the company continues to build out its team ahead of the LEVEL data readout.

news.headline: Tenax and Orion Secure Long-Term Levosimendan Supply Deal, Extend License Term to 2035
news.summary: Tenax and Orion Corporation signed a five-year Supply Agreement (with auto-renewals) naming Orion as primary manufacturer of oral levosimendan, and executed a Sixth Amendment to their 2013 License Agreement extending the deadline for U.S. regulatory approval of the product to December 31, 2035.

news.headline: Guggenheim Raises Tenax Therapeutics Price Target to $40 from $34, Maintains Buy
news.summary: Guggenheim raised its price target on TENX to $40 from $34 while maintaining its Buy rating, and increased its estimated probability of Phase 3 LEVEL trial success to 80% from 75% ahead of the August data readout.

news.headline: Piper Sandler Raises Tenax Therapeutics Price Target to $50 from $20 on Trial Data Outlook
news.summary: Piper Sandler more than doubled its price target on TENX to $50 from $20 while maintaining an Overweight rating, citing the upcoming Phase 3 LEVEL trial results for TNX-103 expected in the third quarter of 2026.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Announces Results of 2026 Annual Meeting
news.summary: At its June 2, 2026 annual meeting, Tenax stockholders elected all seven director nominees (including CEO Michael Davidson and founder Stuart Rich) to one-year terms and ratified Cherry Bekaert LLP as independent auditor for fiscal 2026.

catalysts.label: Late-breaking presentation of Phase 3 LEVEL data at ESC Congress 2026
catalysts.detail: Tenax will present full Phase 3 LEVEL trial results (TNX-103 oral levosimendan in PH-HFpEF) in a Late-Breaking Clinical Science session, scheduled 11:15-11:30 CEST on August 29, 2026 in Munich.

catalysts.label: Topline Phase 3 LEVEL trial data readout
catalysts.detail: Topline results on the primary endpoint (change in 6-minute walk distance) from the ~230-patient, fully randomized registrational LEVEL trial are expected, following database lock and statistical analysis.

catalysts.label: Completion of Phase 3 LEVEL-2 trial enrollment
catalysts.detail: Global enrollment in the second registrational Phase 3 study (LEVEL-2) of oral levosimendan in PH-HFpEF is ongoing and anticipated to complete by end of 2027.

catalysts.when: H2 2026 / early 2027 (contingent on positive LEVEL data)
catalysts.label: Potential NDA submission for TNX-103 (oral levosimendan)
catalysts.detail: If Phase 3 LEVEL data are positive, Tenax is expected to move toward an FDA New Drug Application submission for TNX-103 in PH-HFpEF; no formal filing date has been announced.

pulse: Tenax Therapeutics is in the final stretch before its defining catalyst: topline Phase 3 LEVEL data for TNX-103 (oral levosimendan) in PH-HFpEF, now guided for August 2026 with a late-breaking presentation at the ESC Congress in Munich on August 29. The company has fully randomized its ~230-patient trial, secured its manufacturing and IP runway via an amended long-term supply and license deal with Orion (U.S. approval deadline extended to 2035), and held about $118.8 million in cash as of Q1 2026, seen funding operations into Q1 2028. Analyst sentiment has grown increasingly bullish heading into the readout (Piper Sandler PT $50, Guggenheim PT $40, consensus Strong Buy), though the binary trial outcome remains the dominant swing factor for the stock.
